en: And so the way that we did that was really by starting with the jobs to be done for our community and our advertisers.
zh: 所以我们这样做的方式，实际上是从为我们的社区和广告商完成的任务（jobs to be done）开始的。

speaker: Unknown
en: So getting really, really basic and straightforward on, okay, for Snap-chatters, our jobs to be done, it's as simple as like get people to download the app, get them to add their close friends, get them to, I don't know, use lenses, something like that.
zh: 所以变得非常非常基础和直接，好的，对于 Snap-chat 用户来说，我们要完成的任务，就像让人们下载应用，让他们添加亲密朋友，让他们，我不知道，使用镜头（lenses），诸如此类。

speaker: Unknown
en: On the advertiser front, right?
zh: 在广告商方面，对吧？

speaker: Unknown
en: It could be bringing people into the ad platform, configuring their campaign, et cetera.
zh: 这可能是让人们进入广告平台，配置他们的活动等等。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so by listing out all these jobs to be done, really for the community journey and for advertisers as well, it became very clear where we could use agents, where we needed to be very focused in terms of building cross-functional teams around those jobs supported by AI tools.
zh: 所以通过列出所有这些要完成的任务，真的针对社区旅程和广告商，变得非常清楚我们在哪里可以使用智能体（agents），我们需要在哪里非常专注，围绕这些任务构建跨职能团队，并由 AI 工具支持。

speaker: Unknown
en: And it's also given us, I think, a really helpful mechanism to track our progress against the business outcomes for each of those jobs.
zh: 我认为这也给了我们一个非常有用的机制，来跟踪我们在每个任务的业务结果方面的进展。

speaker: Unknown
en: So I think for us trying to, while I think in this moment of time, you certainly want to have a thousand flowers bloom and people are building agents and experimenting, I think at the same time, making sure that we stay focused on what matters to our community, what matters to advertisers is really, really important.
zh: 所以我认为对于我们来说，试图……虽然我认为在这个时刻，你当然想让百花齐放，人们正在构建智能体并进行实验，但我认为同时，确保我们保持专注于对社区重要的事情，对广告商重要的事情，真的非常重要。

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay.
zh: 好的。

speaker: Unknown
en: I want to take a step back and think about, just kind of reflect back.
zh: 我想退一步，思考一下，只是回顾一下。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's been 15 years since you've been working on Snap.
zh: 你在 Snap 工作已经 15 年了。

speaker: Unknown
en: You launched about 15 years ago.
zh: 你大约在 15 年前推出了它。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm curious just what are some of the maybe biggest lessons, maybe the hardest lessons you've learned over the past 15 years, things that you're like, "Oh, I didn't realize this as I was starting this company."
zh: 我很好奇，在过去的 15 年里，你学到的一些最大的教训，也许是最难的教训是什么，那些你会说，“哦，我在创办这家公司时并没有意识到这一点”的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah, I think it's hard to comprehend how much the job changes over time.
zh: 是的，我认为很难理解这份工作随着时间推移发生了多大的变化。

speaker: Unknown
en: I mean, it's really quite extraordinary to go from my job in the early days with Bobby and I was helping design the product, but I was also answering customer support emails.
zh: 我的意思是，从我和 Bobby 在早期的日子里的工作到现在，确实非常非凡，当时我正在协助设计产品，但我也在回复客户支持邮件。

speaker: Unknown
en: I was trying to get our legal filing sorted out.
zh: 我试图把我们的法律文件整理好。

speaker: Unknown
en: I was trying to raise money.
zh: 我试图筹集资金。

speaker: Unknown
en: As the company grows and things evolve, so much more of the job is about leadership, about making sure you're really developing people, selecting the right people to be on the team, developing the culture, doing that really, really seriously.
zh: 随着公司的发展和环境的变化，工作的更多部分变成了关于领导力，关于确保你真正培养人才，选择合适的人加入团队，发展文化，非常非常认真地做这些事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: And in some ways, creating a structure that can enforce that to have a cohesive culture and a vision around the company, it becomes about strategy, managing through enormous change like what we're seeing with artificial intelligence today, but also several years ago, managing through the pandemic or managing through rebuilding our advertising platform as the landscape changed.
zh: 在某种程度上，创建一个能够强化这种结构的架构以拥有凝聚力的文化和围绕公司的愿景，这就变成了关于战略，管理巨大的变革，就像我们今天在人工智能领域看到的那样，但在几年前，也是在疫情期间管理，或者随着环境变化重建我们的广告平台时管理。

speaker: Unknown
en: So the job has just changed so dramatically.
zh: 所以这份工作变化如此剧烈。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think that's part of what's so energizing about it, what I enjoy so much about it, but it's also something I never in a million years could have anticipated when we were first getting started.
zh: 我认为这也是它如此令人振奋的部分原因，也是我如此享受它的部分原因，但这绝对也是我在一开始时万万没有想到的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: How have you most changed and I guess evolved over that course?
zh: 在那段时间里，你是如何改变和进化的？

speaker: Unknown
en: What's a big area that you've improved in?
zh: 你改进的一个主要领域是什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: One of the big focus areas for me was learning how to communicate more effectively, both to our team, but also here on a podcast or with the world, I mean, so much of the job is about becoming a great communicator.
zh: 我的一个主要关注领域是学习如何更有效地沟通，既对我们的团队，也在这里的播客上或与世界沟通，我的意思是，工作的很大一部分是成为一名伟大的沟通者。

speaker: Unknown
en: I remember I had the opportunity to meet President Clinton at one point in the early days of the company and he was like, "It's very interesting, being president is really being explainer in chief and your job is actually to just explain stuff to people and help them make sense of the world and the company and their role in it."
zh: 我记得在公司早期我有机会见到克林顿总统，他说，“做总统很有趣，其实就是要做首席解释官，你的工作实际上就是向人们解释事情，帮助他们理解世界、公司以及他们在其中的角色。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And I always thought that was really interesting.
zh: 我一直觉得那真的很有趣。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I go back to that a lot.
zh: 我经常回想那件事。

speaker: Unknown
en: So much of my job is to explain things to our company and to our shareholders, to the world in ways that really make sense and can inspire folks and help us to all move in the right direction.
zh: 我的工作很大一部分是以真正有意义的方式向我们的公司、我们的股东和世界解释事物，能够激励大家并帮助我们都朝着正确的方向前进。

speaker: Unknown
en: So I think communication just becomes such a core skill.
zh: 所以我认为沟通只是成了一项核心技能。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think in the early days, I was unnecessarily dismissive of the really important role of communication.
zh: 我认为在早期，我不必要地忽视了沟通的非常重要的作用。

speaker: Unknown
en: For founders that are hearing this and they're like, "Okay, I'm going to work on this," What most helped you level up in this space?
zh: 对于听到这个并且想，“好吧，我要在这方面努力”的创始人来说，什么最帮助你在这一领域提升？

speaker: Unknown
en: Is it like coaches?
zh: 是像教练吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Is it people around you?
zh: 是你身边的人吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Is it just doing it?
zh: 就是做这件事吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Just doing it is really the only way.
zh: 去做真的是唯一的途径。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I remember that was really the advice one of our board members gave to me because I was so reluctant.
zh: 我记得那真的是我们的一位董事会成员给我的建议，因为我是如此不情愿。

speaker: Unknown
en: I didn't want to do an all hands.
zh: 我不想做全员大会。

speaker: Unknown
en: I didn't want to do Q&A.
zh: 我不想做问答。

speaker: Unknown
en: In the early days of the company, I was like, "I'll just send an email."
zh: 在公司的早期，我是想，“我就发一封邮件。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And he's like, "What are you doing?"
zh: 他说，“你在做什么？”

speaker: Unknown
en: He's like, "Too bad, Evan.
zh: 他说，“太糟糕了，Evan。

speaker: Unknown
en: This is your job."
zh: 这是你的工作。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think that was really, really good advice.
zh: 我认为那真的是非常好的建议。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so literally I was like, "Okay, fine.
zh: 所以真的我当时想，“好吧，行。

speaker: Unknown
en: This is a core part of my job and what I need to learn how to do.
zh: 这是我工作的核心部分，也是我需要学会做的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm going to learn to love it.
zh: 我要学着爱上它。

speaker: Unknown
en: I want to love it.
zh: 我想爱上它。

speaker: Unknown
en: I want to get up there and get in front of our company and really enjoy answering all these really tough questions and digging into the business with our team."
zh: 我想站到那里，站在公司面前，真正享受回答所有这些非常棘手的问题，并与我们的团队一起深入业务。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And now I really do.
zh: 现在我真的做到了。

speaker: Unknown
en: I've learned to really enjoy that dialogue with our team.
zh: 我已经学会了真正享受与我们团队的这种对话。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I learned so much from the questions that folks ask.
zh: 我从人们问的问题中学到了很多东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I love that openness.
zh: 我喜欢这种开放性。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think it's just such an important part of our company and who we are.
zh: 我认为这只是我们公司和我们是谁的如此重要的一部分。

speaker: Unknown
en: I had Rahul Vohra on the podcast a while back, the founder of Superhuman.
zh: 前段时间我请了 Rahul Vohra 上播客，Superhuman 的创始人。

speaker: Unknown
en: I don't know if this is what you went through, but he went through a transition where he loved what he was doing and then ended up being pushed by VCs and leaders to like, okay, you got to do these other things.
zh: 我不知道你是否经历过这种经历，但他经历了一个转变，他喜欢他正在做的事情，然后被风投和领导者推动，比如，好吧，你必须做这些其他事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then like, okay, I really don't like this and I'm going to find a way to do the thing I really enjoy because that's going to reflect in so many ways across the business and help us succeed."
zh: 然后就像，好吧，我真的不喜欢这个，我要找到一种方法去做我真正享受的事情，因为这会以多种方式反映在整个业务中并帮助我们成功。”

speaker: Unknown
en: Is that something you went through, where just like, okay, I just want to get back to the product and building?
zh: 这是你经历过的事情吗，就像，好吧，我只想回到产品和构建上？

speaker: Unknown
en: I think that's only partly true if you want to stay in the CEO job.
zh: 我认为如果你想留在 CEO 的工作岗位上，这部分只有一部分是正确的。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think the CEO job has a lot of requirements that are not necessarily things that you love to do in the morning.
zh: 我认为 CEO 工作有很多要求，这些不一定是你在早上喜欢做的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's a huge amount of responsibility that comes along with being CEO.
zh: 担任 CEO 伴随着巨大的责任。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's a lot of operational rigor that I think needs to be developed.
zh: 我认为需要发展大量的运营严谨性。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think not every part of the CEO job is glamorous.
zh: 我认为不是 CEO 工作的每个部分都很光鲜。

speaker: Unknown
en: So I think if I just wanted to work on product, I would pick a different job at Snap.
zh: 所以我想，如果我只是想做产品，我会在 Snap 选择一份不同的工作。

speaker: Unknown
en: But I think because I really like leading our company and leading the direction and strategy for the business, and because I see a huge opportunity in transforming the way that people use computing in the future, both through Snapchat, but also with specs, I think this is the right role for me right now.
zh: 但我想，因为我真的很喜欢领导我们的公司，领导业务的方向和战略，而且因为我看到在未来改变人们使用计算方式的巨大机会，既通过 Snapchat，也通过 specs，我认为这是目前适合我的角色。

speaker: Unknown
en: I mean, certainly I would spend more time with my family and probably have more fun on some days if I was in a different role or more focused on design.
zh: 我的意思是，当然，如果我在不同的职位，或者更专注于设计，我会花更多时间陪伴家人，在某些日子也会更有乐趣。

speaker: Unknown
en: But I think the opportunity for a business like ours at the scale that we have, it's a once in a lifetime opportunity for me.
zh: 但我认为，对于我们这样规模的企业来说，这对我来说是一次千载难逢的机会。

speaker: Unknown
en: And despite its challenges, I do like the CEO job.
zh: 尽管有挑战，我确实喜欢 CEO 这份工作。

speaker: Unknown
en: So kind of along those lines, you put out this letter, I think it was the end of last year where you described this coming year as the crucible moment for Snap.
zh: 所以顺着这些思路，你发布了这封信，我想是去年年底，你在信中把这一年描述为 Snap 的「关键时刻」（crucible moment）。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's so interesting just like you guys have a billion monthly active users.
zh: 有趣的是，你们有 10 亿月活跃用户。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's just absurd.
zh: 这太荒谬了。

speaker: Unknown
en: I don't think people realize this.
zh: 我不认为人们意识到了这一点。

speaker: Unknown
en: You're making six billion a year in revenue, something like that.
zh: 你们每年创造 60 亿美元的收入，大概是这样。

speaker: Unknown
en: One of the very few social networks that lasts that is durable with a very valuable, interesting audience.
zh: 你是为数不多的能够持久生存、具有韧性的社交网络之一，拥有非常有价值、有趣的受众。

speaker: Unknown
en: On the flip side, the stock hasn't been killing it.
zh: 但另一方面，股票表现并不好。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's like these investors coming at you trying to tell you to change all these things.
zh: 有这些投资者来找你，试图告诉你改变所有这些东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: I guess just thoughts, reflections, and what do you think people are missing about where this might go?
zh: 我想问问你的想法、反思，你认为人们对于未来的走向遗漏了什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: Well, that's really one of the reasons why I called this year a crucible moment.
zh: 嗯，这就是我之所以称今年为「关键时刻」（crucible moment）的原因之一。

speaker: Unknown
en: I mean, the company's almost at the scale of entering the Fortune 500, which is really exciting.
zh: 我的意思是，公司的规模几乎要进入财富 500 强了，这非常令人兴奋。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's almost at a billion monthly active users.
zh: 它几乎有 10 亿月活跃用户。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's about to launch specs after 12 years of investment in this future computing platform, but at the same time, it's still not net income profitable, for example, because we've been investing so heavily in the future and making the choice to do that.
zh: 它即将在投资了这个未来计算平台 12 年之后推出 specs，但与此同时，例如它还没有实现净利润盈利，因为我们一直在对未来进行如此大量的投资，并做出这样做的选择。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so I think this is the year that we have to prove that Snapchat can be a really strong, profitable business, that it continues to grow both in terms of the reach of our audience and their engagement with new products, whether it's topic chats or spotlight, or I think we've got 200 million people playing games every month on Snapchat now.
zh: 所以我认为今年是我们必须证明 Snapchat 可以成为一个真正强大、盈利的业务的年份，它将继续增长，无论是从我们受众的触达还是他们与新产品的互动来看，无论是 topic chats 还是 spotlight，或者我想我们现在有 2 亿人在 Snapchat 上每月玩游戏。

speaker: Unknown
en: So gaming is becoming a big part of the engagement drivers on Snapchat.
zh: 所以游戏正在成为 Snapchat 上互动驱动因素的重要组成部分。

speaker: Unknown
en: And the reason why that's so important is that it's going to be very hard for us to win long-term in specs without a really solid foundation.
zh: 这之所以如此重要的原因在于，如果没有一个真正坚实的基础，我们要在 specs 长期获胜将会非常困难。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so I think we need to demonstrate that after a couple years of rebuilding our ad platform, rebuilding our go to-market efforts, really accelerating the growth of the small medium customer part of our business, which has grown really rapidly for us that this year we have a really solid foundation to launch the next chapter in the company's history.
zh: 所以我认为我们需要证明，在重建我们的广告平台、重建我们的上市努力几年之后，真正加速我们业务中中小客户部分的增长，这对我们来说增长非常快，即今年我们拥有一个非常坚实的基础来推出公司历史的下一章。

speaker: Unknown
en: So it's a real turning point for Snap that's really exciting, but it's definitely an all hands on deck moment for the company.
zh: 所以这对 Snap 来说是一个真正的转折点，非常令人兴奋，但这绝对是公司的一个全员时刻（all hands on deck moment）。

speaker: Unknown
en: You had a really interesting way of describing Snap in the market.
zh: 你有一种非常有趣的方式在市场上描述 Snap。

speaker: Unknown
en: You called Snap the middle child.
zh: 你称 Snap 为「中间的孩子」（middle child）。

speaker: Unknown
en: Talk about just what that means.
zh: 谈谈这意味着什么。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think one of the things that's so funny about our service is that while we're very, very large in terms of the scale of our audience, in terms of the scale of our business, we're much larger than a Pinterest or Reddit, for example, we're also way smaller than Meta and Google.
zh: 我认为关于我们的服务，最有趣的事情之一是，虽然就受众的规模而言我们非常非常大，就业务的规模而言，我们比 Pinterest 或 Reddit 大得多，例如，我们也比 Meta 和 Google 小得多。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so we occupy this very interesting sort of middle position in the market.
zh: 所以我们占据了市场上这种非常有趣的中间位置。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think that comes with a lot of benefits because we're big enough.
zh: 我认为这带来了很多好处，因为我们足够大。

speaker: Unknown
en: We have enough scale to do really interesting things, but it also comes with the challenges of being overshadowed in some cases by our older brothers and maybe sometimes the younger kids are getting more attention.
zh: 我们有足够的规模来做真正有趣的事情，但它也带来了一些挑战，比如在某些情况下被我们的哥哥姐姐掩盖了光芒，也许有时年纪更小的孩子得到了更多的关注。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so I think again, as in this crucible moment, it's part of defining who we are as a company and who we are as a business.
zh: 所以我认为同样，在这个「关键时刻」（crucible moment）中，这是定义我们作为一家公司是谁、我们作为一项业务是谁的一部分。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think one of the things that will play an important role in that is getting specs out to consumers because I think right now we've talked so long about the role that specs will play in the world.
zh: 我认为其中一个将发挥重要作用的事情是将 specs 推向消费者，因为我认为现在我们已经谈论了 specs 将在世界中扮演的角色这么久了。

speaker: Unknown
en: We've worked on developing the platform for so long, but without something that you can really play with and hang onto and use yourself, it's hard to really understand the next chapter of Snap's journey.
zh: 我们致力于开发这个平台这么久，但如果没有一些你可以真正玩耍、坚持和亲自使用的东西，很难真正理解 Snap 旅程的下一章。

speaker: Unknown
en: So I think one of the biggest challenges that middle children face is defining themselves, standing out from their older siblings and younger siblings.
zh: 所以我认为中间的孩子面临的最大挑战之一是定义自己，从哥哥姐姐和弟弟妹妹中脱颖而出。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so I think this is a moment and a year when Snap is really going to define itself, and I'm really excited about that.
zh: 所以我认为这是一个 Snap 真正将要定义自己的时刻和一年，我对此感到非常兴奋。

speaker: Unknown
en: Speaking of middle child, you have a number of children and I'm curious, there's a whole going in a whole different direction.
zh: 说到「中间的孩子」，你有几个孩子，我很好奇，这里要往一个完全不同的方向走了。

speaker: Unknown
en: What's your just policy on screens and devices?
zh: 你对屏幕和设备的政策是什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: Having built Snapchat and then also specs, just how do you think about devices and your kids?
zh: 既然构建了 Snapchat，还有 specs，你是如何看待设备和你的孩子的？

speaker: Unknown
en: Well, we've got a real range.
zh: 嗯，我们的范围真的很广。

speaker: Unknown
en: We've got four boys, two, six, seven, and 15.
zh: 我们有四个男孩，分别是 2 岁、6 岁、7 岁和 15 岁。

speaker: Unknown
en: So we approach it very differently for all the kids at different ages.
zh: 所以我们对所有不同年龄的孩子采用非常不同的方法。

speaker: Unknown
en: For the two-year-old, it's like zero screen time.
zh: 对于 2 岁的孩子，屏幕时间为零。

speaker: Unknown
en: We really just want to essentially read with him and play, explore outside and those sorts of things, but not really focus on screens unless he is getting a haircut because he really struggles with getting his haircut.
zh: 我们真的只想基本上和他一起阅读、玩耍、在户外探索以及诸如此类的事情，而不是真的专注于屏幕，除非他理发，因为他真的很难理发。

speaker: Unknown
en: So when he gets a haircut, he really loves Bobcat tractors, those four-wheel sort of Bobcat tractors, and he loves to watch YouTube videos of people driving Bobcat tractors around.
zh: 所以当他理发时，他真的很喜欢山猫（Bobcat）拖拉机，那种四轮类型的山猫拖拉机，他喜欢看人们开着山猫拖拉机转圈的 YouTube 视频。

speaker: Unknown
en: So when he gets his haircut, he can watch people drive Bobcat tractors.
zh: 所以当他理发时，他可以看人们开山猫拖拉机。

speaker: Unknown
en: But other than that, no screen time.
zh: 但除此之外，没有屏幕时间。

speaker: Unknown
en: For the six and seven-year-olds, they are, I would say, infrequent movie watchers.
zh: 对于 6 岁和 7 岁的孩子，他们是，我会说，不常看电影的人。

speaker: Unknown
en: But other than that, again, we don't give them phones or anything like that.
zh: 但除此之外，同样，我们不给他们手机或类似的东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: In fact, it was really fun over the holidays for Christmas, I gave them that new ...
zh: 事实上，在圣诞节的假期里真的很有趣，我给了他们那个新的……

speaker: Unknown
en: I think Palmer Lucky has a startup where they are sort of remaking Gameboys.
zh: 我觉得 Palmer Lucky 有一家初创公司，他们在某种程度上重新制作 Gameboys。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah, I have one of those.
zh: 是的，我有一个那样的东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah, it's really fun.
zh: 是的，真的很有趣。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Lenny
en: Mod retro.
zh: Mod retro。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah, mod retro.
zh: 是的，mod retro。

speaker: Unknown
en: So the TBPN guys gave me two of them for our kids for Christmas.
zh: 所以 TBPN 的人给了我们两个，作为给孩子们的圣诞礼物。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so they get to spend a little time on those in addition to watching movies every now and then.
zh: 所以他们除了偶尔看电影，还可以花点时间玩那个。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then with the 15-year-old, he is all in on technology, whether he's using it for school or whether he's using it to talk with his friends or using Snapchat.
zh: 至于那个 15 岁的孩子，他全身心投入技术，不管是用它来学习还是用它和朋友聊天或使用 Snapchat。

speaker: Unknown
en: So it really depends on kids and where they're at developmentally, but I think for us, that's been our approach.
zh: 所以这真的取决于孩子以及他们的发展阶段，但我认为对我们来说，这就是我们的做法。

speaker: Unknown
en: Is there anything you're doing with AI in your kids, maybe even the older one, just AI education sort of tools, ChatGPT?
zh: 关于你在孩子身上有什么用 AI 的做法吗，哪怕是那个大一点的孩子，比如 AI 教育类的工具，ChatGPT？

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah, it's so funny.
zh: 是的，太搞笑了。

speaker: Unknown
en: At the breakfast table the other morning, actually, I was just asking them a lot about how are their teachers talking about AI at school?
zh: 实际上就在前几天吃早餐的时候，我就在问他们很多关于老师在学校里怎么谈论 AI 的问题？

speaker: Unknown
en: How are they thinking about integrating in the curricula Are you guys using AI all the time?
zh: 他们怎么考虑将其整合进课程大纲里？你们 guys 一直在用 AI 吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think the answer so far has been not really, which I think is interesting, but certainly our kids play around with it at home.
zh: 我认为目前的答案是并没有，我觉得这很有意思，当然我们的孩子们在家里会摆弄它。

speaker: Unknown
en: Our beta testers for specs, which is really fun.
zh: 他们是 specs 的 beta 测试员，真的很有趣。

speaker: Unknown
en: I really love using the glasses together with them and some of the really cool experiences there.
zh: 我真的很喜欢和他们一起戴眼镜，以及那里的一些非常酷的体验。

speaker: Unknown
en: And Flynn, who's the eldest is probably most fluent in using AI tools.
zh: Flynn，是最大的那个，可能在使用 AI 工具方面最流利。

speaker: Unknown
en: But what I think is so fun, as a kid, I think all of us as kids are incredibly imaginative.
zh: 但我觉得最有趣的是，作为孩子，我觉得我们所有人作为小孩子都是难以置信的富有想象力。

speaker: Unknown
en: We're all born creative.
zh: 我们生来就有创造力。

speaker: Unknown
en: We have all these amazing ideas to share.
zh: 我们有这么多分享出去的惊人想法。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think what's so cool about AI is how in nearly an instant, you can take any idea you have and make something.
zh: 我认为 AI 酷的地方在于，几乎在瞬间，你可以拿任何你有的想法并做出一些东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so I think that's so empowering for young people.
zh: 所以我认为这对年轻人来说是非常赋予能力的。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's certainly an experience I want our kids to have.
zh: 这当然是我希望我们的孩子拥有的体验。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think it's going to be so important to deeply integrate into the way that education works today.
zh: 我认为深入整合到今天的教育运作方式中将是如此重要。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's interesting.
zh: 很有意思。

speaker: Unknown
en: Snapchat has always been really good at allowing you to create and feel like a superstar, just create all these really crazy lenses and filters and all these things.
zh: Snapchat 一直非常擅长让你去创作并且感觉像个超级巨星，只需要创建所有这些非常疯狂的镜头和滤镜以及所有这些东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: And it's interesting AI, just like vibe coding just unleashes so much creativity.
zh: 而且 AI 很有意思，就像 vibe coding 一样释放了如此多的创造力。

speaker: Unknown
en: And kids are so imaginative already.
zh: 孩子们已经非常有想象力了。

speaker: Unknown
en: Absolutely.
zh: 绝对的。

speaker: Unknown
en: So interesting.
zh: 太有意思了。

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay.
zh: 好的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Well, to close out our conversation, I've got a couple.
zh: 好的，为了结束我们的对话，我有几个问题。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm going to take us to a couple corners, recurring corners on the podcast.
zh: 我要带我们去几个角落，播客上的常驻角落。

speaker: Unknown
en: One is AI corner.
zh: 一个是 AI 角落。

speaker: Unknown
en: So the question is, what's a way you use AI in your, let's say, work that is just interesting that might be interesting for other people to learn from?
zh: 所以问题是，你在比如说工作中使用 AI 的一种方式是什么，那可能是有趣的，其他人可能会从中学习到有趣的地方？

speaker: Unknown
en: Well, one of the things that I really love about AI, at Snap, I've got access to all of our dashboards and documents and data.
zh: 嗯，关于 AI 我真的很喜欢的一件事是，在 Snap，我有权访问我们所有的仪表盘、文档和数据。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so we have Glean that integrates all this data for me.
zh: 所以我们有 Glean 为我整合所有这些数据。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I've built just an agent that will go and comb through everything that's happening in the company and let me know what's up, what I need to focus on, what I need to catch.
zh: 我只是建立了一个智能体，它会去梳理公司里发生的每一件事，并让我知道发生了什么，我需要关注什么，我需要抓住什么。

speaker: Unknown
en: And every now and then it really catches something that I hadn't realized or need to focus a bit more on.
zh: 时不时地，它真的会发现一些我没意识到的事情，或者我需要多关注一点的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so I think we're just so fortunate to have so much structured data in our company.
zh: 所以我认为我们非常幸运，在公司里有这么多结构化的数据。

speaker: Unknown
en: I mean, for our leaders, they also send me every week the three things from the week and three things looking ahead.
zh: 我是说，对于我们的领导者，他们每周也发给我过去一周的三件事和前瞻的三件事。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so I can very, very easily get a sense for the hotspots in the company, the priority areas, and combined with all of our dashboards and metrics about the way that people are using our service.
zh: 所以我可以非常非常容易地了解公司的热点、优先领域，并结合我们所有的仪表盘和关于人们使用我们服务方式的指标。

speaker: Unknown
en: I feel like I have a really good sense for what's happening in the company.
zh: 我觉得我对公司里发生的事情有很好的感觉。

speaker: Unknown
en: So I think what's been most exciting for me in terms of AI is that it's enabling, I think, the type of leadership structure that I've always wanted, which is very flat, very fast moving.
zh: 所以我认为对我来说，在 AI 方面最令人兴奋的是，它实现了，我认为，我一直想要的那种领导结构，那就是非常扁平、非常快速的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think having an AI copilot to help me do that's been a big deal.

speaker: Unknown

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah.

speaker: Unknown
en: I mean, that I've done in Glean, which is great.
zh: 我的意思是，我在 Glean 做过这个，这很棒。

speaker: Unknown
en: Obviously you can build all sorts of stuff in Cloud, but Glean for us is the most secure way to access all of our different documents and dashboards and things like that.

en: Is there anything interesting in your AI stack?

speaker: Unknown

en: I think one of the things that we're thinking a lot about is just taking the entire workflow and enabling it with an agent.
zh: 我认为我们正在思考很多事情之一，就是接管整个工作流并用一个智能体来启用它。

speaker: Unknown
en: So for example, taking a product idea and with our go to-market agent, taking that product idea, writing the spec, identifying the relevant folks who need to be involved in sign-offs and understanding it, actually helping to do the risk analysis on the product from a legal, trust and safety perspective, writing the go to-market materials like the blog, et cetera.
zh: 例如，采纳一个产品创意，用我们要 go to-market 智能体，采纳那个产品创意，编写规格，识别需要参与审批和理解它的相关人员，实际上帮助从法律、信任和安全角度对产品进行风险分析，编写 go to-market 材料，比如博客等等。

speaker: Unknown
en: We're working now on actually building the visuals as well, but doing that in one shot is really wild.
zh: 我们现在正在致力于构建视觉素材，但一次性做完所有这些真是太疯狂了。

speaker: Unknown
en: So I think there's all sorts of examples like that across the company in terms of the way that agents can play a really massive role in changing the way that we work.
zh: 所以我认为在公司内部有各种各样的这样的例子，关于智能体可以在改变我们要工作方式方面发挥非常大的作用。

speaker: Unknown
en: And that's connected to how we think about those jobs to be done.
zh: 这与我们要如何思考那些需要完成的任务联系在一起。

speaker: Unknown
en: So if we can define a job to be done clearly enough that we can build an agent to do it, that can really create a lot of lift.
zh: 所以如果我们能够足够清晰地定义一个需要完成的任务，以至于我们可以构建一个智能体来完成它，那真的可以产生很大的提升。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah.

speaker: Unknown
en: Sweet.

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay.

speaker: Unknown
en: I'll take us to another recurring corner in the podcast, Contrarian Corner.

speaker: Unknown
en: What's something that you believe that a lot of other people don't believe?
zh: 有什么是你相信而很多人不相信的？

speaker: Unknown
en: What's kind of a contrarian take about anything that comes to mind?
zh: 有什么反向的观点，关于任何想到的事情？

speaker: Unknown
en: Well, I think for me, we're in an industry where so much of the conversation is focused on technology.
zh: 嗯，我认为对我个人来说，我们身处这样一个行业，其中很多对话都集中在技术上。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think maybe my contrarian point of view or my contrarian angle is humanity is far more important than the technological developments, largely because humanity dictates how technology is adopted.
zh: 我认为也许我的反向观点或反向角度是，人性远比技术发展更重要，很大程度上因为人性决定了技术如何被采用。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think for example, right now, people are massively underestimating the role that human adoption and human comfort with advances in artificial intelligence will determine its deployment.
zh: 我认为例如，现在，人们大大低估了人类采用和人类对人工智能进步的舒适度将决定其部署的作用。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think technology leaders think that folks will just blindly adopt new technology as it comes out.
zh: 我认为技术领导者认为人们会在新技术推出时盲目地采用它。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think we're going to enter a period of time where there's going to be a huge amount of societal pushback on a lot of the changes that are coming with AI.
zh: 我认为我们将进入一个时期，对于伴随 AI 到来的许多变化，会有大量的社会阻力。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so I think a lot of our focus as an industry, but more broadly in the world needs to be putting humanity first, making sure that the tools we're developing are advancing humanity's goals in addition to business goals.
zh: 所以我认为我们作为一个行业，但更广泛地说在世界上的大量重点，需要是把人性放在首位，确保我们要开发的工具在推进商业目标的同时也推进人性的目标。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think in our industry at least, that's a bit contrarian.
zh: 我认为至少在我们要行业里，这有点反向。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And just so we don't need to go too down this path, but it's so hard for the AI labs, I think, to on the one hand be like, okay, this is really dangerous if it gets much smarter and we need people to know this because this is happening and moving quickly.
zh: 只是所以我们不需要在这个话题上走得太远，但这对于 AI 实验室来说太难了，我认为，一方面说，好吧，如果它变得更聪明这就真的很危险，我们需要人们知道这一点，因为这正在发生并且发展得很快。

speaker: Unknown
en: On the other hand, how do you not just freak everyone out and make everyone so afraid of where things are going?
zh: 另一方面，你怎么能不让所有人都吓坏了，让所有人都对事情的发展方向感到如此恐惧？

speaker: Unknown
en: It's like, well, what do you do?
zh: 就像是，嗯，你能做什么呢？

speaker: Unknown
en: What's the right thing for them to do?
zh: 他们做什么是正确的？

speaker: Unknown
en: And because of all that doom and gloom, I'm sure you've seen these surveys.
zh: 因为所有的那些厄运和阴霾，我确定你看过这些调查。

speaker: Unknown
en: AI is below ICE in terms of popularity and below Iran.
zh: AI 在受欢迎程度上低于 ICE，也低于 Iran。

speaker: Unknown
en: This isn't going great.
zh: 这进展得不顺利。

speaker: Unknown
en: I don't know.
zh: 我不知道。

speaker: Unknown
en: I don't know what I would do if I were the lab.
zh: 我不知道如果我是实验室我会怎么做。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I love, I guess that's like a benefit of Snapchat is it makes like you guys have been in AI for a very long time and it shows you the playfulness of it all and it doesn't have to be that serious.
zh: 我喜欢，我想那是 Snapchat 的一个好处，就是它表明你们已经涉足 AI 很长时间了，它展示了所有这些的趣味性，而不必那么严肃。

en: And what does that mean for the way that we build things and organize ourselves as a society?

speaker: Unknown

speaker: Unknown
en: Awesome.

en: Wow.

en: All right.

zh: 显然，我认为人们对人工智能将改变我们社会的方式有很多担忧，但我认为有很多值得乐观的地方，就计算变得更人性化而言，而这真的是我们要关注的。

speaker: Unknown
en: So really excited to share more about all the stuff we've been working on, later this year.
